rationale: Tehran's contradiction of Trump's Iran deal claim is a fresh domestic credibility story, while the China espionage arrest of a US academic enters its second cycle; both sustain moderate domestic political and geopolitical relevance for US residents.

top_events.title: Tehran says 'nothing' finalised after Trump claims Iran deal near / Flip flop or deliberate?
top_events.summary: Iran publicly contradicting Trump's deal announcement is a fresh domestic credibility and foreign policy story; raises questions about US negotiating strategy and Middle East stability relevant to oil markets and US voters.

top_events.title: China arrests US academic at conference for 'espionage activities'
top_events.summary: Second cycle of China detaining a US researcher signals sustained US-China tension; directly affects Americans traveling to or working with China and feeds into broader trade/security policy debate.

top_events.title: Toronto police officer shot while investigating US consulate attack
top_events.summary: An attack on a US consulate in Canada is a minor but direct security incident involving US diplomatic infrastructure; no major escalation but warrants monitoring for US residents.
